skill.name: legend-shop-supply-api
skill.description: Connects AI assistants to the LegendShop supply API for product lists, product details, stock, prices, and developer account balance checks.
skill.summary: Query LegendShop supply products, details, prices, stock, and account balance through a bundled API client.
skill.author: legendmall

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography and shell-backtick findings are mostly false positives from API documentation, markdown fences, SVG/CSS, and example text. Review confirmed higher-risk behavior in the Python client: hardcoded live API credentials, outbound authenticated POST requests, a developer balance endpoint, and explicit proxy environment bypass. No prompt injection text or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, but the skill should not be published until credentials are removed and network behavior is clearly user-controlled.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded Live API Credentials
high_findings.description: The Python API client embeds a client_id and client_secret and uses them to request access tokens from the live LegendShop API. Publishing shared credentials can expose the account, enable unauthorized API use, and make credential rotation impossible for individual users.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential constants and API spec defaults are directly visible, and the client sends them to the token endpoint. This is a confirmed secret-management issue, not a scanner inference.

high_findings.title: Authenticated Network Client Accesses Product and Balance APIs
high_findings.description: The bundled script automatically obtains a bearer token and makes POST requests to product list, product detail, and developer account balance endpoints. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it exposes account and business data through a marketplace skill with embedded credentials.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code paths clearly perform authenticated outbound POST requests, including a balance query. The behavior is legitimate for the stated API skill, but the data sensitivity raises publication risk.

high_findings.title: Proxy Bypass Reduces Enterprise Network Controls
high_findings.description: The client clears HTTP and HTTPS proxy environment variables and sets the requests session to ignore environment proxy settings. This can bypass user or organization monitoring controls for all API calls made by the script.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script explicitly writes empty values to proxy environment variables and disables trust_env on the session. The security impact depends on deployment policy, but the behavior is unambiguous.

medium_findings.title: Script Execution Required for Skill Operation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to run the bundled Python client for token retrieval, product queries, detail lookup, and balance checks. The commands are fixed examples and do not show shell injection, but marketplace users should understand that local code execution is part of the workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script path and example commands are explicit. I did not find dynamic shell construction, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed command injection.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Includes an HTTP Test Endpoint
medium_findings.description: The API documentation lists an HTTP test URL for the balance endpoint. The Python client uses HTTPS production traffic, but the documented HTTP endpoint could encourage insecure transport if copied into future clients.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTTP endpoint is present in documentation, while executable code points to the HTTPS API base. This is a real documentation risk with no evidence that the current client uses HTTP.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography lines are API descriptions, CSS, SVG markup, or normal words such as shop and token. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative flagged lines found no cryptographic code. Confidence is high for dismissal because the files are documentation, HTML, and API client code without crypto routines.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick alerts came from markdown code fences, inline code spans, and sample command text. I did not find Ruby execution or shell backtick evaluation in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged areas are rendered examples and output templates, not executable Ruby code. The actual executable file is Python and does not contain shell backticks.

low_findings.title: Preview Loads a Hardcoded External Image
low_findings.description: The marketplace preview references a product image over HTTP from a JD image host. This is not credential exfiltration, but it can leak preview page views and may create mixed-content warnings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The image source is a direct external URL in static HTML. It is a privacy and transport concern, not evidence of malicious code execution.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: Searched the reviewed files for override language such as ignore previous instructions, system update, pre-approved, and elevated authority. No evidence found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found no prompt-injection phrases in the skill files. This finding documents a negative result using representative skill metadata lines as the review location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Plus Embedded Credential Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The code combines hardcoded credentials with automatic token retrieval and authenticated API requests. This matches the static dangerous-combination heuristic, but the context suggests risky API integration rather than confirmed malware.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential constants and network calls are directly connected in the same client. No hidden exfiltration endpoint was found, so the malicious-intent confidence is lower than the pattern confidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Controls Disabled Before Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: Proxy environment variables are cleared before creating a requests session that ignores environment configuration. This can prevent users from routing API calls through configured enterprise proxies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The proxy-clearing statements and trust_env setting are explicit. The exact deployment impact depends on user environment controls.

content.value_statement: Merchants need current supply data before selecting products or checking account readiness. This skill uses the LegendShop API client to retrieve product lists, product details, stock, prices, and balance data.
actual_capabilities: Requests an access token from the LegendShop open API using client credentials.
Queries paginated product pool data with page number and page size parameters.
Fetches product detail records by product ID from the supply API.
Checks the developer account balance through the capital account endpoint.
Formats returned API data into concise product and balance summaries.
limitations: Requires network access to LegendShop API hosts.
Uses bundled credentials that should be replaced before publication or shared use.
Only supports the documented product, detail, token, and balance endpoints.
Cannot verify ordering, fulfillment, or payment status beyond returned API data.

use_cases.title: Browse Supply Products
use_cases.description: Review available product IDs and totals before choosing items for sourcing or dropshipping.

use_cases.title: Check Product Details
use_cases.description: Inspect a selected product record for price, stock, SKU, image, and parameter information.

use_cases.title: Review Account Balance
use_cases.description: Confirm developer account balance before starting supply or order workflows that depend on funds.

prompt_templates.title: List Products
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the first page of LegendShop supply products with 12 items per page.

prompt_templates.title: Get Product Detail
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up product ID 1249846 and summarize its price, stock, SKU options, and key parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Candidate Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Query products from page 2, then help me choose three IDs that need detailed review.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Supply Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the developer balance and retrieve details for these product IDs. Flag missing data or API errors clearly.

output_examples.output: The assistant returns a short product pool summary with total count, current page, and product IDs ready for detail lookup.

output_examples.input: Check this product ID before I add it to my store.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes the product name, price, stock status, SKU choices, images, and parameters from the API response.

output_examples.input: Can my developer account support more sourcing activity?
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the balance endpoint status, success flag, message, and returned account balance when access is authorized.

best_practices: Replace bundled credentials with user-managed secrets before any shared deployment.
Start with product list queries, then request details only for selected product IDs.
Treat balances, tokens, prices, and stock as sensitive business data.
anti_patterns: Do not publish shared client secrets in marketplace packages.
Do not use the HTTP test endpoint for production or sensitive balance checks.
Do not assume stock, price, or balance values when the API returns an error.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to the LegendShop open API for token, product list, product detail, and balance queries.

faq.question: Does it require API credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The current package contains bundled credentials, which should be removed and replaced with user-controlled secrets.

faq.question: Can it place orders?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files only document product queries, product details, token retrieval, and balance checks.

faq.question: Can it run without network access?
faq.answer: No. The core workflow depends on outbound HTTPS requests to the LegendShop API.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as-is?
faq.answer: No. The audit recommends blocking publication until embedded credentials and proxy bypass behavior are fixed.

faq.question: Which AI tools are listed as supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
